title: Manforgon

{{Quote|I'm Imperial Customs Officer Tellix. What was your last port of call and what business do you have on Manforgon?|Tellix, to a group of spacers|<ref name="CRB">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]''</ref>}}
'''Manforgon''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]]. It was used for [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] as a [[Prison/Legends|penal]] [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region. Eventually becoming a lawless world fought over by [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]], Manforgon was turned over to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] by the [[Ancient Houses|noble houses]] of the Senex-Juvex. The Empire used the planet for military purposes, and during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] [[Customs official/Legends|Officer]] [[Tellix]] served there.

==Description==
Manforgon was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Manforgon system/Legends|Manforgon system]] of the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], a part of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The [[Senex-Juvex Loop]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] linked it to the [[Mussubir system/Legends|Mussubir system]] as well as the [[Kassido system/Legends|Kassido system]] of the neighboring [[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex sector]]. Manforgon was a dull, brownish gray–[[Color/Legends|colored]] world.<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
{{Quote|Easy, friend. We've traveled here from Tatooine with a cargo of sand wine. Is there a problem?|Anderun, to Imperial Customs Officer Tellix|<ref name="CRB" />}}
For [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]], Manforgon was used as a [[Prison/Legends|penal]] [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] that held [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] from the entire Senex-Juvex region. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], Manforgon became a lawless planet, with [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] fighting each other over control. Eventually, Manforgon came to be frequently used as a setting in the Senex-Juvex region's romances, which would begin with either an illegitimate [[Youngling/Legends|child]] or a framed [[Noble/Legends|noble]] being abandoned on the planet, only to triumphantly return from it later.<ref name="TEA" />

At some point following the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="TEA" /> in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government demanded that the [[Lord/Legends|lords]] of the aristocratic [[Ancient Houses]] of the Senex-Juvex surrender the control of a world in the region for use by the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]]. The nobles complied and indifferently ceded Manforgon. Shortly thereafter, the planet had become known as one of the least pleasant duty posts in the Empire.<ref name="TEA" />

{{RPG}}
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Tellix]] held the post of [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] [[Customs official/Legends|Officer]] on Manforgon. At one point, he approached a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] that had landed on the planet, inquiring as to the vessel's point of origin and its [[Spacer/Legends|crew's]] business on Manforgon. One of the crewmembers, [[Anderun]], then claimed that the starship had traveled from<ref name="CRB" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] planet<ref name="TEA" /> [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] with a cargo of a beverage known as [[sand wine]].<ref name="CRB" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
Among the prisoners on Manforgon were members of the [[Hutlarian]] [[Species/Legends|species]] as well as [[Human/Legends|Humans]], including those [[Aquellan|hailing from]] the world of [[Aquella]]. Adventurous Senex-Juvex men sometimes landed on Manforgon incognito in order to boost their reputations.<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Manforgon was first mentioned in the [[2000]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook|core rulebook]] for the [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the planet is described as the setting of a hypothetical [[roleplaying game]] adventure. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="CRB" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed it in grid square L-17.<ref name="TEA" />
